page: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 virtual machine belonging to the compute-focused FS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, alongside a 50 MB L3 cache, 32 KB L1d cache, and 0.25 MB L2 cache. Local storage consists of an 8 GB volume, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud hardware. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1037.99 is categorized as weak, while the multi-core score of 2045.39 falls into the poor category. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block speeds at 14.46 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 11.46 GB/sec. Computational tasks such as Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores reflect these hardware constraints, placing the instance in the bottom 10% for image processing, text processing, and software compilation.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the F2s is positioned as an entry-level compute-focused instance. However, its older Intel Xeon architecture and low memory capacity present significant tradeoffs for modern workloads. It is best suited for lightweight, non-memory-intensive tasks such as basic static web serving, small-scale database operations, or low-demand utility scripting. It can handle basic single-threaded tasks like small model LLM inference but is unsuitable for larger machine learning models or memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 virtual machine within the compute-focused FS family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, 4.0 GB of memory, and an 8 GB local storage volume with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance lacks GPU accelerators and public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth is constrained, with large-block read speeds at 14.46 GB/sec. Consequently, the F2s is best suited for lightweight, non-memory-intensive workloads such as basic static web serving, minor database operations, or low-demand utility scripting where dedicated CPU allocation is required on a constrained budget.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 VM with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 CPU. It offers entry-level compute performance for lightweight web serving and basic database tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for entry-level compute workloads.
tagline: An entry-level, compute-focused virtual machine with dedicated Intel processors and premium storage support for lightweight, non-memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor at 2.3 GHz
Configured with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 8 GB of local storage
Premium Storage capable for enhanced disk performance options
Delivers entry-level compute performance suitable for basic static web serving
Best fit for lightweight utility scripting and small-scale database operations
